requirement.original: 现在的push和merge以及pull没有像commit一样的agentic智能判断机制，给我方案
requirement.clarified: Add dedicated analyst subagents for /push, /merge, and /pull commands. Each gets a pre-execution (or post-execution for pull) intelligent judgment mechanism mirroring the /commit + changelog-analyst architecture. Deliver a design plan (spec + context); implementation follows separately.
requirement.what: Three new analyst agent files (push-analyst, merge-analyst, pull-analyst) + modifications to three command bodies to dispatch them
requirement.why: User wants the same agentic safety-and-intelligence layer that /commit has via changelog-analyst applied to the other three git workflow commands

included: agents/push-analyst.md — new git-read-only analyst (filesystem writes restricted to /tmp/agentic-commit/push-analyst/) with grant-write capability
agents/merge-analyst.md — new git-read-only analyst (filesystem writes restricted to /tmp/agentic-commit/merge-analyst/) with grant-write capability
agents/pull-analyst.md — new git-read-only advisory analyst (no filesystem write; advisory stdout report only)
commands/push.md — add pre-execution dispatch steps + grant validation; commits-to-push range must use refs/remotes/<resolved_remote>/<branch>..HEAD
commands/merge.md — add pre-execution dispatch steps + grant validation + tip revalidation
commands/pull.md — add pre_pull_head capture + post-execution conditional dispatch; dispatch pull-analyst whenever HEAD changed (even on stash-pop failure exit 1)
policies/tool-policy.v1.json — add push-analyst, merge-analyst, pull-analyst role entries (M10; required for policy_registry to permit dispatch)
hooks/pretool-cp-checkin.py — add three role names to CP_AGENTS set (M10)
excluded: hooks/push.sh — wrapper script unchanged per architect constraint
hooks/merge.sh — wrapper script unchanged per architect constraint
hooks/pull.sh — wrapper script unchanged per architect constraint
hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py — no new patterns per architect constraint

success_criteria: push-analyst is dispatched before push.sh; verdict=blocked prevents push; commits-to-push range uses refs/remotes/<resolved_remote>/<branch>..HEAD (not @{u}..HEAD)
merge-analyst is dispatched before merge.sh; verdict=blocked prevents merge; grant expires in 60s
pull-analyst is dispatched whenever HEAD changed (even on pull.sh non-0 exit due to stash-pop failure); report is advisory only
All three analyst agents have explicit DO NOT sections forbidding git write ops
Grant tokens are nonce-keyed, session-scoped, state-bound, and single-use
hooks/push.sh, hooks/merge.sh, hooks/pull.sh are byte-for-byte unchanged
push-analyst, merge-analyst, pull-analyst registered as named roles in policies/tool-policy.v1.json and added to CP_AGENTS in hooks/pretool-cp-checkin.py (M10)
constraints: Gates live in command body .md files, not in wrapper scripts (architect constraint)
Each command gets a dedicated analyst agent — no shared fat agent (architect constraint)
pretool-git-privilege-guard.py must not be modified (architect constraint)
push-analyst grant is Chain B; push-gate token is Chain A — both must pass independently
merge-analyst grant must expire in 60s (merge state freshness)
pull-analyst produces no grant and blocks nothing — pure advisory
policy_registry.py:72-81 hard-denies any role not in policies/tool-policy.v1.json — all three analyst roles MUST be registered there before dispatch

in_user_path: policies/tool-policy.v1.json (to modify — add push-analyst, merge-analyst, pull-analyst role entries per M10)
hooks/pretool-cp-checkin.py (to modify — add three role names to CP_AGENTS set per M10)
path_dependent_shared_infra: hooks/push.sh (read for context; architecture understanding; remote resolution logic at lines 38-42 is the reference for push-analyst; not modified)
hooks/merge.sh (read for context; architecture understanding; not modified)
hooks/pull.sh (read for context; architecture understanding; stash-pop-after-HEAD-change sequence at lines 154-175 is the reference for pull-analyst trigger logic; not modified)
agents/changelog-analyst.md (read as pattern reference; not modified)
hooks/lib/policy_registry.py (read for context; lines 72-81 get_role_policy returns hard-deny for unknown roles; lines 269-270 return (False, 'unknown role <role>'); not modified)

out_of_scope_observations.observation: pull.sh exits 0 on the stale-lock cancellation path (user chooses not to remove lock file). This exit-0 path represents a cancelled/no-op pull where no commits were fetched. The pull-analyst skip-if-HEAD-unchanged logic (AC7) handles this correctly as a side effect — HEAD will not change on a cancelled pull — but the exit code is still misleading. Not in scope for this cycle.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: User observes /push, /merge, /pull have no intelligent judgment step; only /commit has this via changelog-analyst
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: New feature: the three command bodies were written before the agentic-analyst pattern was established for /commit. No analyst agent files were created for them.
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: N/A — original commands pre-date the changelog-analyst pattern
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Without agentic review, push/merge/pull execute with no semantic risk assessment: sensitive files in commits-to-push are not flagged; branch divergence anomalies are not surfaced before merge; post-pull semantic risk (schema changes, API surface changes) is invisible
observations: commands/push.md lines 1-93: no analyst dispatch found (inferred: Read tool)
commands/merge.md lines 1-51: no analyst dispatch found (inferred: Read tool)
commands/pull.md lines 1-45: no analyst dispatch found (inferred: Read tool; wc -l confirmed 45 lines)
agents/ directory listing: no push-analyst.md, merge-analyst.md, pull-analyst.md present (inferred: Glob tool)
policies/tool-policy.v1.json:792-818: changelog-analyst role entry present; push-analyst, merge-analyst, pull-analyst role entries absent (inferred: Read tool)
hooks/lib/policy_registry.py:72-81: get_role_policy returns {_unknown_role: True, default_action: deny} for absent roles (inferred: Read tool)
hooks/lib/policy_registry.py:269-270: is_allowed returns (False, 'unknown role <role>') for unknown roles — hard deny (inferred: Read tool)
hooks/pretool-cp-checkin.py:60-72: CP_AGENTS set contains changelog-analyst but not push-analyst, merge-analyst, pull-analyst (inferred: Read tool)
hooks/push.sh:38-42: remote resolution is fork-prefer-origin — 'fork' if git remote get-url fork succeeds, else 'origin'; explicit arg overrides (inferred: Read tool)

hooks/pull.sh:120-175: git pull --rebase at line 120; success message at line 154; stash pop at lines 158-176; stash pop failure exits 1 at line 175; HEAD already changed before stash pop runs (inferred: Read tool)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred only — this is a new-feature request, not a bug)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Requirement is a feature gap, not a runtime symptom. Verified by reading all three command files and listing agents/ directory.
root_cause_analysis.located_source: commands/push.md, commands/merge.md, commands/pull.md — all lack analyst dispatch

git_bisect_result.bisect_blocked: N/A — new feature request, not regression

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: push和merge以及pull没有像commit一样的agentic智能判断机制
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: Each of /push, /merge, /pull dispatches a dedicated analyst subagent that produces a structured verdict before (or after for pull) the wrapper script executes
BA spec + context JSON covering all architecture decisions produced; implementation follows in a separate dev cycle

development_approach.strategy: Create three analyst agent files following the changelog-analyst pattern, then update the three command bodies to insert dispatch steps with proper grant validation. Wrappers remain unchanged.
files_to_modify: policies/tool-policy.v1.json (M10: add push-analyst, merge-analyst, pull-analyst role entries following changelog-analyst format at lines 792-818)
hooks/pretool-cp-checkin.py (M10: add push-analyst, merge-analyst, pull-analyst to CP_AGENTS set at lines 60-72)
development_approach.validation_approach: QA verifies: (1) analyst dispatch step present in each command body; (2) grant paths and validation logic correct; (3) wrapper scripts byte-for-byte unchanged; (4) DO NOT sections present in each analyst agent; (5) policies/tool-policy.v1.json has all three new role entries; (6) policy_registry.is_allowed('push-analyst', 'Write', '/tmp/agentic-commit/push-analyst/x') returns (True, 'ok'); (7) push-analyst uses refs/remotes/<resolved_remote>/<branch>..HEAD not @{u}..HEAD; (8) pull-analyst dispatched on HEAD change regardless of pull.sh exit code

push_analyst_design.trigger: Pre-execution, dispatched from commands/push.md before calling push.sh
analysis_scope: commits-to-push range: refs/remotes/<resolved_remote>/<branch>..HEAD (where resolved_remote is the fork-prefer-origin result matching push.sh:38-42: 'fork' if git remote get-url fork succeeds, else 'origin', or explicit arg). NOT @{u}..HEAD — @{u} may track a different remote (e.g., upstream origin in a fork workflow). For first push where no remote tracking ref exists (refs/remotes/<resolved_remote>/<branch> absent), use all commits in git log HEAD.
file names in committed blobs for sensitive patterns (.env, *.key, *.pem, *password*, *secret*, *credential*)
ahead/behind count vs resolved remote tracking ref (not vs @{u})
branch name vs protection rules (direct push to main/master)
divergence check between resolved remote and local HEAD

grant_schema.head_sha: git rev-parse HEAD at analysis time

push_analyst_design.grant_consumption: single-use; unlinked by commands/push.md on successful validation
orchestrator_steps: Step 0: Parse ARGUMENTS, resolve REMOTE (fork-prefer-origin logic matching push.sh:38-42: if git remote get-url fork succeeds use 'fork' else 'origin'; explicit arg overrides)
Step 1: Validate push-gate token (Chain A — existing logic, unchanged)
Step 2: Compute pre-push snapshot: pre_head, branch, resolved_remote, remote_url; generate request_id nonce
Step 3: Dispatch push-analyst subagent with branch, pre_head, remote_name, remote_url, request_id
Step 4: Read grant at nonce path; validate all fields; consume (unlink); if verdict=blocked abort; if verdict=warn display risks and proceed
Step 5: Call push.sh <remote> (unchanged — also consumes Chain A push-gate token)

authorization_chains.chain_a: push-gate token at /tmp/agentic-commit/push/<repo-hash>/<branch>.json — SHA identity (did changelog-analyst produce this commit?); consumed by push.sh
authorization_chains.chain_b: push-analyst grant at /tmp/agentic-commit/push-analyst/<repo-hash>/<session-id>/<nonce>.json — content review (did analyst inspect what is being pushed?); consumed by commands/push.md

merge_analyst_design.trigger: Pre-execution, dispatched from commands/merge.md before calling merge.sh
analysis_scope: overnight-state consistency validation (read only; NOT for branch selection)

grant_schema.source_tip: git rev-parse refs/heads/<resolved_branch> at analysis time
grant_schema.default_tip: git rev-parse refs/heads/<default_branch> at analysis time

merge_analyst_design.grant_consumption: single-use; unlinked by commands/merge.md on successful validation
merge_analyst_design.tip_revalidation: commands/merge.md re-reads source_tip and default_tip immediately before calling merge.sh; if either changed since grant was written, abort and require re-run
orchestrator_steps: Step 0: Resolve branch name (existing logic — from overnight-state or explicit arg; NO filesystem guessing)
Step 1: Compute snapshot: source_tip, default_branch, default_tip; generate request_id nonce
Step 2: Dispatch merge-analyst subagent with resolved_branch, source_tip, default_branch, default_tip, request_id
Step 3: Read grant at nonce path; validate all fields including expiry (60s); consume (unlink); if verdict=blocked abort
Step 4: Revalidate branch tips (re-read current source_tip and default_tip); abort if changed
merge_analyst_design.expiry_rationale: 60s because merge state changes rapidly — another commit or push in that window invalidates the analysis

pull_analyst_design.trigger: Post-execution, dispatched from commands/pull.md after pull.sh returns when pull_exit_phase is 'success' (exit 0, HEAD changed) OR 'stash_restoration_failed' (exit non-0, no rebase state dir, HEAD changed). NOT dispatched when pull_exit_phase is 'rebase_conflict' (rebase state dir present, or no HEAD change).
analysis_scope: merge commits in range (non-linear history signal)
dependency manifest changes (package.json, requirements.txt, go.mod, Cargo.toml, pyproject.toml)
orchestrator_steps: Step 0: Capture pre_pull_head = git rev-parse HEAD

Step 2: Capture post_pull_head = git rev-parse HEAD (ALWAYS — pull.sh prints 'Pull completed successfully' at line 154 and advances HEAD BEFORE stash pop at lines 157-174; stash pop failure exits 1 at line 175 but HEAD already changed)

Step 3: Determine pull_exit_phase: exit 0 -> 'success'. If exit non-0: check for .git/rebase-merge or .git/rebase-apply directory. If rebase state dir exists -> 'rebase_conflict' (git rebase partially applied commits before conflict; HEAD may have changed; stash pop was never reached). If no rebase state dir AND pre_pull_head != post_pull_head -> 'stash_restoration_failed' (rebase fully succeeded, HEAD advanced, then stash pop failed at line 174). If no rebase state dir AND pre_pull_head == post_pull_head -> 'rebase_conflict' (conservative fallback for unknown pre-rebase failure).
Step 4: If pre_pull_head == post_pull_head: if exit_code != 0 display pull.sh error; else print 'no HEAD change (no-op pull)'; stop (no analyst dispatch)
Step 5: Dispatch pull-analyst subagent with pre_pull_head, post_pull_head, branch, pull_exit_phase
Step 6: Display pull-analyst report to user (report includes stash-restoration-failure warning when pull_exit_phase='stash_restoration_failed')

pull_exit_phase_enum.success: exit 0 — git pull --rebase succeeded and stash pop (if any) succeeded
pull_exit_phase_enum.rebase_conflict: exit non-0 AND (.git/rebase-merge OR .git/rebase-apply exists) — git rebase stopped mid-operation due to conflict; HEAD may or may not have changed; stash pop was never reached. Also used as conservative fallback when no rebase state dir exists AND HEAD unchanged.
pull_exit_phase_enum.stash_restoration_failed: exit non-0 AND no rebase state directory exists AND HEAD changed — git pull --rebase fully succeeded (HEAD advanced past line 154 success message), then stash pop failed at pull.sh line 174; local changes remain in stash@{0}

pull_analyst_design.pull_exit_phase_discriminant: The rebase-state-directory (.git/rebase-merge or .git/rebase-apply) is the PRIMARY discriminant, not HEAD equality. Both rebase-conflict and stash-pop-failure paths can produce 'exit non-0 + HEAD changed'. The key difference: rebase conflict leaves rebase state dirs behind; stash pop failure does not (rebase fully completed before stash pop ran).
pull_analyst_design.stash_pop_failure_rationale: pull.sh verified sequence (lines 119-175): git pull --rebase runs at line 120; rebase conflict exits at line 151 BEFORE stash pop (lines 158-174). On successful rebase, 'Pull completed successfully' printed at line 154; stash pop then runs at lines 158-174; stash pop failure exits 1 at line 174. When rebase conflicts, git may partially apply commits, changing HEAD and leaving .git/rebase-merge or .git/rebase-apply. Using rebase-state-directory presence as primary discriminant: if .git/rebase-merge or .git/rebase-apply exists after non-zero exit -> rebase_conflict (regardless of HEAD change). If no rebase state dir exists AND HEAD changed -> stash_restoration_failed (rebase completed fully; only stash pop failed). HEAD equality alone is an unreliable discriminant because partial rebase can change HEAD.
pull_analyst_design.toctou_rationale: Pre-analysis is inherently stale because the pull itself changes what is incoming. Post-execution advisory satisfies the user requirement for 'intelligent judgment' without introducing false gates on stale pre-fetch analysis.

required_sections: DO NOT section (FORBIDDEN operations — must include: no git commit, push, merge, pull, rebase, reset --hard, branch -D, force ops, writing grants for other agents)

setup.reason: non-UI -- agent-prompt edit; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths

Analyst subagent: reads commits-to-push range, writes nonce-keyed grant token
imports_from: leaf — reads git state, writes /tmp grant

component_chain.role: Orchestrator body: branch resolution, analyst dispatch, grant validation + tip revalidation, merge.sh call
Analyst subagent: reads branch divergence, checks conflict markers, validates overnight-state consistency, writes nonce-keyed grant token (60s expiry)
imports_from: leaf — reads git state, writes /tmp grant
component_chain.evidence: Absent: agents/ Glob showed no merge-analyst.md

component_chain.role: Orchestrator body: pre_pull_head capture, pull.sh call, HEAD-change detection, pull-analyst dispatch
Advisory-only analyst subagent: reads new-commits range, flags semantic risks, outputs report (no grant written)
imports_from: leaf — reads git state, outputs to stdout

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Guards against push without a prior /commit in this session

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: _evaluate_push() — env + grant manifest validation for git push
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Blocks unauthorized agent git push via Bash tool
Blocks unauthorized agent git merge unless wrapper env is set
Blocks agent git pull from running directly

policy_registration.enforcer_hard_deny_lines: 72-81 (get_role_policy returns {_unknown_role: True} for absent roles); 269-270 (is_allowed returns (False, 'unknown role <role>'))

push-analyst.note: No broad /tmp/* or /var/tmp/* — write access restricted to role-specific grant directory only. /tmp/agentic-commit/push-analyst/* covers the nonce-keyed grant files.

merge-analyst.note: No broad /tmp/* or /var/tmp/* — write access restricted to role-specific grant directory only.

pull-analyst.note: pull-analyst writes no grant and is advisory-only. Write tool EXCLUDED to enforce this contract — prevents any accidental grant writes. No allowed_write_path_prefixes needed.

policy_registration.negative_ac: policy_registry.is_allowed('push-analyst','Write','/tmp/other/x.json') must return (False,...). policy_registry.is_allowed('pull-analyst','Write','/tmp/agentic-commit/pull-analyst/x.json') must return (False,...) because Write not in allowed_tools.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex identified 13 issues across the draft architecture: 4 blockers (grant not enforced in wrapper, state not bound, collision-prone paths, merge tips not bound) and 8 majors (single-use not specified, wrong remote analyzed, wrong file surface reviewed, branch inference duplication, pull advisory vs gate ambiguity, unreliable HEAD@{1}, exit-0 ambiguity, agent files missing). 1 observation about /tmp grant security model.

findings.issue: Analyst gate is at the markdown/orchestrator layer, but wrappers remain unchanged — push.sh does not validate push-analyst grant
findings.proposed_fix: Put grant validation in commands/push.md body (orchestrator reads grant before calling push.sh). Codex correctly noted wrappers are unchanged; the gate IS in commands/push.md. Applied: spec now explicitly states commands/push.md Step 4 reads and consumes the push-analyst grant before Step 5 calls push.sh.
findings.rationale: The gate was already intended to be in commands/push.md per architect constraint; spec now makes this explicit with numbered steps

findings.issue: Grant freshness relies on expiry only, not state binding
findings.proposed_fix: Bind each grant to: repo hash, session_id, nonce, branch, current HEAD, selected remote URL/ref for push; source branch tip and default branch tip for merge
findings.rationale: Grant schemas now include head_sha, remote_url for push-analyst and source_tip, default_tip for merge-analyst; tip revalidation step added before merge.sh

findings.issue: Grant paths are collision-prone — branch name used as filename, no nonce/session scoping
findings.proposed_fix: Use /tmp/agentic-commit/<kind>/<repo-hash>/<session-id>/<nonce>.json — include branch as data, not path authority
findings.rationale: Grant paths updated to include session-id and nonce; branch stored as grant content, not path component

findings.issue: Stale grants can be reused — draft does not define single-use consumption
findings.proposed_fix: Command reads exact nonce grant, validates, then unlinks before execution. Missing/malformed/expired = abort
findings.rationale: Spec now explicitly states single-use (unlink on successful validation) for both push-analyst and merge-analyst grants

findings.issue: Push analyst may analyze the wrong remote target
findings.proposed_fix: Resolve remote using same logic as push.sh (fork-prefer-origin) before dispatch; pass to analyst; bind grant to remote name + URL
findings.rationale: Orchestrator Step 0 now resolves REMOTE using fork-prefer-origin logic before dispatch; remote_name and remote_url passed to analyst and bound in grant

findings.issue: Push sensitive-file review inspects git status (working tree) rather than commits-to-push
findings.proposed_fix: push-analyst must review the ahead range being published: upstream..HEAD or all commits for first push
findings.rationale: push-analyst analysis scope updated to 'commits-to-push range: upstream..HEAD (or all commits for first push)'

findings.issue: Merge analyst duplicates branch inference — can conflict with /merge contract
findings.proposed_fix: Resolve branch once in commands/merge.md; pass exact branch to merge-analyst; analyst validates only
findings.rationale: Spec now states: branch resolved once in commands/merge.md Step 0; merge-analyst receives it as input and may read overnight-state files for consistency VALIDATION ONLY, not for branch selection

findings.issue: Merge grant not bound to source/default branch tips
findings.proposed_fix: merge-analyst records source_tip and default_tip; command revalidates both immediately before merge.sh runs
findings.rationale: merge-analyst grant schema now includes source_tip and default_tip; commands/merge.md Step 4 re-reads both tips and aborts if changed

findings.issue: Pull post-only analysis may not satisfy 'agentic judgment' if judgment is expected to gate risk
findings.proposed_fix: Either explicitly define pull-analyst as advisory-only, or use two-phase (fetch first, then analyze, then pull)
findings.rationale: Adopted advisory-only recommendation. Spec explicitly states pull-analyst is pure advisory with no grant and no gate. Two-phase option documented as a future enhancement.

findings.issue: Pull range HEAD@{1}..HEAD is unreliable — reflog entries can be affected by stash pop, rebase steps
findings.proposed_fix: commands/pull.md should capture pre_pull_head = git rev-parse HEAD before pull.sh; pass explicit pre_pull_head and post_pull_head to pull-analyst
findings.rationale: commands/pull.md Step 0 now captures pre_pull_head before pull.sh; post_pull_head captured after; both passed to pull-analyst

findings.issue: Pull exit code 0 does not always mean new pulled changes — stale-lock cancellation also exits 0
findings.proposed_fix: Run pull-analyst only if pre_pull_head != post_pull_head
findings.rationale: commands/pull.md Step 4 compares pre_pull_head with post_pull_head; pull-analyst skipped if equal

findings.issue: New analyst agents missing from architecture — no agent files exist yet
findings.proposed_fix: Add separate single-purpose agent definitions with explicit DO NOT sections
findings.rationale: Already in scope as M1/M2/M3; confirmed as required by this finding. DO NOT sections are now AC9.

findings.issue: /tmp grants are coordination artifacts, not strong security boundaries — agents can write under /tmp
findings.rationale: This matches the existing commit grant model (same /tmp approach). Goal is workflow discipline, not cryptographic enforcement. Documented in spec but not actioned.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 12 in-scope findings (4 blockers, 8 majors) were applied in the original cycle. QA retry (2026-05-17): 5 QA objections addressed. Codex adversarial review (2026-05-17): 5 Codex findings processed — (1) pull-analyst Write tool removed from role entry + negative ACs added + broad /tmp/* narrowed to role-specific /tmp/agentic-commit/<role>/* (applied: in_scope_real_bug); (2) grant path session_id/repo_hash orchestrator-pass noted — already in Architecture Design dispatch inputs, no spec change needed (in_scope_minor, existing spec adequate); (3) context JSON pull_analyst_design.trigger corrected from 'exits 0 AND HEAD changed' to 'regardless of exit code, if HEAD changed' (applied: in_scope_real_bug); (4) stash_restoration_failed boolean replaced with pull_exit_phase enum: success/rebase_conflict/stash_restoration_failed (applied: in_scope_minor, improves precision); (5) settings.json hint corrected — no settings.json agent allow-list change required (applied: in_scope_real_bug).
